name: MCP Kali Server
what_it_does: Lightweight API bridge that connects Claude Desktop or other AI clients to a Kali Linux machine, enabling AI-assisted command execution for penetration testing, CTF challenges, and security reconnaissance using tools like Nmap, Metasploit, and sqlmap.
use_cases: AI-guided CTF challenge solving with real-time command execution on Kali
Penetration testing reconnaissance automation (Nmap scans, enum4linux, Gobuster)
Bug bounty hunting workflow assistance with AI suggesting and running recon commands
HackTheBox / TryHackMe machine exploitation with AI-driven tool selection
Security training environments where an AI coach can demonstrate tool usage
not_for: Production security operations or enterprise SOC environments
Users who need authentication or access controls on command execution
Any use against systems you do not own or have explicit written permission to test
best_when: You are running ethical penetration testing or CTF challenges in a controlled environment with a dedicated Kali Linux instance, and want AI to suggest and execute recon/exploitation commands iteratively.
avoid_when: You need any form of access control, audit logging, or safe command sandboxing. The lack of authentication makes this unsuitable for shared or production environments.

auth.notes: No authentication mechanism is implemented. Security relies entirely on network isolation (default localhost binding) and SSH tunneling for remote access. Anyone who can reach port 5000 can execute arbitrary commands.

pricing.notes: Open source. Requires Kali Linux instance (self-hosted).

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Minimal error handling documentation; structured output for API responses mentioned but not detailed.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Command execution is inherently non-idempotent; no safeguards documented.
known_agent_gotchas: CRITICAL: No authentication — anyone who can reach port 5000 has arbitrary command execution on the Kali machine
Binding to 0.0.0.0 for remote access exposes an unauthenticated command execution endpoint to the network
No command sandboxing, allowlist, or rate limiting on what commands can be executed
Ethical and legal risk: AI agents could execute commands against out-of-scope targets without operator awareness
SSH tunnel requirement for remote use adds operational friction
